extraction.one_line_summary: STUYVEN Jasper won from a small break, outsprinting LAMPAERT Yves after KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren was dropped on the final 2km attack on Bosberg.
tactical_patterns: VAN AVERMAET Greg and BENOOT Tiesj both missed the decisive split despite being favorites, forcing futile late chasing.
LAMPAERT Yves led out the two-up sprint but STUYVEN closed the door, suggesting sitting second is critical on Ninove finish.
DECEUNINCK-QUICKSTEP placed DECLERCQ Tim and LAMPAERT in break, but neither won showing numerical advantage doesn't guarantee control.
Wind-induced echelons after Leberg climb proved more selective than final Muur-Bosberg combination for favorites group composition.

rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: First season win using relaxed preparation approach, stronger on Muur than expected given Tour de France crash absence.
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: negative: Had tactical position advantage in final sprint but lacked power to hold off STUYVEN on Ninove uphill finish.
rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: neutral: Made decisive break but dropped with 2km remaining when LAMPAERT attacked on Bosberg, held third place.
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: Missed key selection despite attacking to bridge, previous double winner couldn't replicate form when it mattered.
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: negative: Missed decisive split and spent race chasing with VAN AVERMAET, poor positioning cost podium chance.
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: Chased hard from favorites group but never rejoined front three, suggesting insufficient strength for solo bridge efforts.
rider_signals.DECLERCQ Tim: positive: Made front group and took strong fifth place, showing Deceuninck had numbers but wrong finishers.
rider_signals.HAUSSLER Heinrich: neutral: Former runner-up bridged to VAN AERT early but sat up in headwind, conserving rather than committing to doomed move.
